steps.title: Spot the “But” Contrast
steps.content: The opening defines Nixon by his Watergate fame; the underlined sentence begins with “But,” shifting to positive accomplishments instead.

steps.title: Identify the Oversimplification
steps.content: C captures what the underlined sentence accomplishes: it provides information (tribal sovereignty, health care reform) showing that the Watergate-focused perception is an oversimplification. The others misidentify the function—A invokes an explanation, B requires comparing two figures when only one is discussed, and D references associates not mentioned in the underlined sentence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
